opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My face is burning, it's completely covered in greasy yellow flakes, and the itching is driving me mad. It blew up over the last day or two and I'm honestly terrified — could this be lupus, or some kind of severe allergic reaction? I need to know it's not something serious.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The rash flared up dramatically over the last 24 to 48 hours and I can't stop thinking it's something dangerous. I want it looked at and treated tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: flared up hard over the last day or two, though I've had milder patches on and off for a long time
symptom_narrative.duration: acute flare for about 48 hours on a background that comes and goes
symptom_narrative.character: red patches with greasy yellow flakes; the skin burns and itches
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch and burning are really getting to me and I look a mess
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the middle of my face — around my nose and the creases beside it, my eyebrows, and my scalp is shedding flakes everywhere
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it comes and goes and gets worse in flares like this one
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — if anything I've made it worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: I've been scrubbing my face hard with strong soap to get the flakes off, and it's been really stressful at work and freezing and dry outside
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: lots of dandruff from my scalp; no fever, my joints are fine, I'm breathing normally
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: yes, I get flaky greasy patches like this from time to time but never this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm distracted and embarrassed by how my face looks and it's stressing me out

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: recurring flaky, greasy patches on my face and scalp over the years

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; recently under extreme stress at work; home is heavily heated with dry indoor air and it has turned cold outside

objective_data_offered.value: red patches with greasy yellow scales in the creases either side of my nose, on my eyebrows and along the front of my scalp, and it looks the same on both sides of my face
objective_data_offered.timing: right now on the video call

objective_data_offered.value: heavy white-yellow flaking coming off the scalp
no fever, forehead doesn't feel hot to touch, and I don't feel unwell in myself
the flat is heated hard and the air is really dry indoors
